message:
chore(beads): sync issues.jsonl after TRACK_Q/TRACK_R/TRACK_S advances

Small JSONL update captured by `br sync --flush-only` after the
TRACK_Q (bd-aztlm) / TRACK_R / TRACK_S regression-test work in
the preceding commits. Keeps the committed beads snapshot
consistent with the local beads database so `br ready`,
`bv --robot-triage`, and history queries remain accurate for
downstream agents.

3. Keep the budgets ready for implementation and validation.

 {"id":"bd-db300.5.4.3","title":"E4.3: define admission control and tail-latency guardrails","description":"## Goal
Define the admission-control and tail-latency guardrails for the many-core execution pipeline as an explicit decision artifact so throughput gains do not come from uncontrolled queue growth, retry storms, or degraded user-visible tails.

## Why This Matters
The system needs an explicit policy for when to accept more work, when to shed or defer work, when to prefer backpressure over helper-lane expansion, and when to force fallback or safe-mode behavior. These guardrails must incorporate the publication and retry behavior chosen in the metadata-publication track rather than pretending scheduling is independent of read-side truth maintenance.

## Scope
Specify:
- the state space and evidence vector used by the guardrail logic, including queue depth, wake-to-run, retry and fallback rates, tail-latency buckets, and any publication-retry or remote-ownership indicators that materially affect user-visible tails,
- the action set, such as `admit`, `defer`, `apply_backpressure`, `shrink_helper_budget`, `force_safe_mode`, or equivalent,
- a loss matrix or equivalent expected-loss rule that makes the tradeoff between throughput and user-visible tail harm explicit,
- calibration or drift guards for any nontrivial controller logic,
- budgeted mode caps and precise on-exhaustion behavior,
- degraded-mode triggers when tail latency or retry pressure exceeds budget,
- operator-visible telemetry and decision records,
- a timescale-separation statement describing how this controller composes with D1 WAL-policy choices, E6 placement/locality policy, and any later cache/routing controller.

## Required Verification and Logging
- Unit tests for policy evaluation, threshold or hysteresis behavior where retained, counterfactual ranking, calibration-drift trigger logic, and degraded-mode trigger logic.
- Integration tests covering bursty arrivals, helper-lane saturation, publication retry spikes, fallback recovery, and shadow-run equivalence against the deterministic conservative policy.
- A named e2e or operator entrypoint such as `scripts/verify_e4_3_tail_guardrails.sh` that exercises representative workload shapes, records guardrail decisions, and emits first-failure bundles.
- Structured logs exposing at minimum `trace_id`, `scenario_id`, `policy_id`, `decision_id`, `guardrail_id`, `queue_depth`, `wake_to_run_ns`, `retry_rate`, `fallback_rate`, `p50_ns`, `p95_ns`, `p99_ns`, `p999_ns`, `control_mode`, `top_evidence_terms`, `posterior_or_confidence`, `expected_loss`, `counterfactual_action`, `regret_delta`, and chosen action.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The policy states exactly when backpressure beats queue growth or extra helper work.
- Tail guardrails are tied to measurable latency and retry budgets rather than vague operator intuition.
- A deterministic conservative mode and a shadow-run or compare mode are explicit.
- The design is explicitly compatible with the metadata-publication decisions from E3 and names its timescale/interference assumptions relative to D1 and E6.
- Unit, integration, and named e2e verification are part of the bead rather than deferred to later cleanup.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead defines explicit admission thresholds, action choices, latency guardrails, retry/fallback budgets, degraded-mode triggers, and a loss-aware or equivalently explicit decision rule.
2. The policy is verified with unit tests, integration tests, and `scripts/verify_e4_3_tail_guardrails.sh` or equivalent named replay coverage.
3. Structured logs expose queue depth, wake-to-run, retry/fallback rates, tail-latency buckets, policy and decision ids, evidence terms, expected loss, counterfactual action, regret delta, and chosen action.
4. The design explicitly incorporates E3 metadata-publication behavior and names its composition assumptions relative to D1 and E6 instead of treating scheduling in isolation.
5. The result is specific enough that later implementation beads can encode the policy without re-litigating thresholds, fallback behavior, or controller composition safety.","notes":"Future-self intent:
- this bead exists to keep throughput work user-safe by constraining tail behavior, retry storms, and fallback churn
- do not let a controller hide publication-path pathologies behind a nicer average","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:35:09.596708209Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-23T02:22:36.685455653Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T02:22:36.684241262Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","db300-program","db300-track-e","quality-gate","throughput","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:22:47.975688383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:16:10.800457423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T00:07:22.643173755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.5.5","title":"E5: design conflict-topology-aware writer routing and page ownership hints","description":"## Goal
Design conflict-topology-aware writer routing or page-ownership hints that reduce unnecessary same-page fights and remote cache-line ownership bouncing without reintroducing centralized writer scheduling. Scope includes page-color or home-lane hints, conflict-history heuristics, and locality-aware ownership transfer so MVCC can preserve its decentralized advantage under heavier writer counts.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. The design identifies what metadata or heuristics would steer writers away from avoidable page conflicts, remote ownership transfers, or cross-node hot spots.
2. Candidate mechanisms such as hot-page maps, page-color or shard hints, home-lane or home-node ownership, and conflict-recentness heuristics are made explicit.
3. It preserves decentralized MVCC semantics rather than imposing file-level serialization, and stale hints degrade gracefully instead of becoming correctness requirements.
4. The proposal can be benchmarked against the existing conflict profile with conflict-rate, retry-count, and remote-HITM-or-cross-node-traffic evidence.
5. The resulting design or decision record includes an explicit verification plan describing the unit tests, end-to-end scenarios, and logging artifacts required for follow-on implementation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The design identifies what metadata or heuristics would steer writers away from avoidable page conflicts, remote ownership transfers, or cross-node hot spots.
2. Candidate mechanisms such as hot-page maps, page-color or shard hints, home-lane or home-node ownership, and conflict-recentness heuristics are made explicit.
3. It preserves decentralized MVCC semantics rather than imposing file-level serialization, and stale hints degrade gracefully instead of becoming correctness requirements.
4. The proposal can be benchmarked against the existing conflict profile with conflict-rate, retry-count, and remote-HITM-or-cross-node-traffic evidence.
5. The resulting design or decision record includes an explicit verification plan describing the unit tests, end-to-end scenarios, and logging artifacts required for follow-on implementation.","notes":"This bead is about reducing avoidable overlap and topology-unfriendly ownership motion, not about hiding real conflicts or weakening correctness. It should complement MVCC rather than turning into a centralized scheduler.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:05:23.601579516Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T13:43:45.082991704Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","conflict-topology","db300-program","db300-track-e","mvcc","parallelism","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2454,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5","author":"TealStream","text":"TealStream perf investigation 2026-03-18: the flagship collapse still looks like logical disjointness failing to become physical disjointness. `STATE_OF_THE_CODEBASE_AND_NEXT_STEPS.md` and the live engine both point at shared parent/right-edge/allocator/page-1 surfaces, not just late conflict discovery. The `write_page` path still has explicit page-one synthetic tracking (`track_concurrent_conflict_only_page`, `clear_stale_synthetic_pending_commit_surface`), and the state document's own best-current interpretation is “avoid shared structural mutation most of the time, and when unavoidable, keep publish tiny.”

High-EV design direction for E5: push the existing concurrent rowid allocator (`crates/fsqlite-mvcc/src/rowid_alloc.rs`) farther toward physical disjointness by combining rowid-range reservation with page-range / extent runway and home-lane hints. Concretely: reserve growth extents per writer or lane, bias split/slack policy to keep inserts leaf-local longer, and feed conflict-history telemetry into home-lane / ownership hints. This is the shortest path to making `commutative_inserts_disjoint_keys` physically disjoint instead of only SQL-disjoint. Fallback trigger: if retry-cause taxonomy later shows publication/snapshot drift dominating instead of parent/right-edge/page-1, deprioritize extent/home-lane work and move budget back to E2.","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:43:44Z"}]}

Keep the telemetry lightweight enough for hot paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:19:22.350689713Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-18T15:16:18.032298817Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conflict-topology","db300-program","db300-track-e","mvcc","parallelism","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2455,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.1","author":"TealStream","text":"Shared telemetry/TODO checklist for the current pass:
- Reuse the E2.1 instrumentation work to capture conflict-topology signals on the live hot path rather than inventing a parallel telemetry stack.
- Required first-pass signals: tier1 vs tier2 write counts, page-lock wait count/time, BUSY retry count, stale-snapshot rejects, page-one conflict-only tracking count/time, and pending-surface clear count/time.
- Required second-pass signals once this lands: retry-cause attribution by structural/page-one/publication class and lane/home affinity hints derived from the same counters.
- Decision rule for E5 follow-on: if the new metrics show topology/page-one work dominating, prioritize home-lane / extent-runway design; if not, keep the budget behind E2 tiny-publish work.","created_at":"2026-03-18T13:58:28Z"},{"id":2460,"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.1","author":"TealStream","text":"Progress update (2026-03-18, pass 2):
- I retraced the disjoint-writer topology evidence after the failed canonical c8 replay attempts. There is still no fresh artifact pack because `rch` times out at 300s before sync on the exact collapsing cell.
- New bounded code work since the last note stayed in `begin_concurrent.rs`: exact `release_set(...)` now replaces scan-based `release_all(txn_id)` on the begin-concurrent commit/abort/finalize path whenever held-lock state is available, which reduces one fixed-cost coordination tax but does not by itself prove the topology story.
- The topology hypothesis is still supported by the new telemetry plan and the existing archived evidence: page-lock wait and page-one/pending-surface maintenance are now explicit report lanes, which is the measurement surface needed to judge home-lane / extent-runway work once a fresh hot-profile artifact can be captured.
- Remote verification remains blocked by unrelated `fsqlite-vfs` compile drift, so this bead is still at evidence-gathering / steering state rather than a finished topology patch.","created_at":"2026-03-18T15:16:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-db300.5.5.3","title":"E5.3: define benchmark protocol for routing hints versus baseline","description":"## Goal
Define the benchmark and evidence protocol for comparing conflict-topology routing hints against the current baseline so routing ideas are judged on reproducible operator-visible evidence rather than isolated anecdotes.

## Why This Matters
Routing hints change where work runs, which pages collide, which caches own hot metadata, and how retry/fallback behavior manifests. The protocol therefore has to measure not just throughput, but also conflict rate, retry amplification, remote ownership, fairness, and interaction with the metadata-publication plane.

## Scope
Define canonical comparison cases covering:
- baseline without routing hints,
- home-lane or home-node hint variants,
- skewed hot-page contention versus disjoint-key workloads,
- weak-cell and strong-cell concurrency points,
- relevant placement profiles where remote ownership matters.

## Required Verification and Logging
- Unit or harness tests that verify scenario selection and metric collection consistency.
- A named operator entrypoint such as `scripts/verify_e5_3_routing_protocol.sh` that runs the protocol and emits artifact manifests.
- Structured logs and reports containing at minimum `trace_id`, `scenario_id`, `routing_mode`, `placement_profile`, `conflict_rate`, `retry_rate`, `fallback_rate`, `remote_ownership_events`, `ops_per_sec`, `p50_ns`, `p95_ns`, `p99_ns`, and fairness summaries.
- Explicit capture of metadata-publication interactions where routing changes snapshot age, retry count, or visibility handoff cost.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The protocol makes routing-hint comparisons mechanically reproducible.
- Reports include conflict, retry, remote-ownership, fairness, and latency evidence in addition to throughput.
- The protocol is explicitly compatible with the metadata-publication design and logging contracts from E3.
- The result is ready for G4 consumption without a second round of protocol invention.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead defines canonical routing-versus-baseline cases across workload shapes, concurrency points, and relevant placement profiles.
2. `scripts/verify_e5_3_routing_protocol.sh` or an equivalent named entrypoint exists in the plan and emits replayable artifacts.
3. Reports include throughput, latency, conflict rate, retry/fallback rate, remote ownership, and fairness evidence.
4. The protocol explicitly captures metadata-publication interactions rather than treating routing as isolated from E3.
5. The bead is self-contained enough to feed G4 and later routing implementation work without reopening the measurement design.","notes":"User-facing intent:
- routing hints are only worth shipping if operators can tell whether they reduced real conflict and tail pain rather than just moving heat around","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T16:19:50.321147207Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:22:48.913261066Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","conflict-topology","db300-program","db300-track-e","evidence","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:22:48.912026796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.5.6","title":"E6: design CPU-affinity, lane-pinning, and NUMA-local allocation strategy","description":"## Goal
Design the CPU-affinity, lane-pinning, and NUMA-local allocation strategy for the target many-core transaction pipeline, down to CCD/CCX/LLC domains, SMT sibling policy, and home-node ownership. This bead turns the high-level many-core architecture into a concrete placement policy for Threadripper/Xeon-class machines.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The design specifies placement of latency-sensitive and throughput-oriented lanes across NUMA nodes, CCD/CCX/LLC domains, and SMT siblings.
- It makes cross-node traffic, cache-line ownership, remote HITM budgets, and responsiveness tradeoffs explicit rather than implicit.
- It defines local allocator or arena placement and concrete cross-node handoff boundaries.
- The output is concrete enough to guide a prototype or benchmarked implementation pass.
- The resulting design or decision record includes an explicit verification plan describing the unit tests, end-to-end scenarios, and logging artifacts required for follow-on implementation.","acceptance_criteria":"- The design specifies placement of latency-sensitive and throughput-oriented lanes across NUMA nodes, CCD/CCX/LLC domains, and SMT siblings.
- It makes cross-node traffic, cache-line ownership, remote HITM budgets, and responsiveness tradeoffs explicit rather than implicit.
- It defines local allocator or arena placement and concrete cross-node handoff boundaries.
- The output is concrete enough to guide a prototype or benchmarked implementation pass.
- The resulting design or decision record includes an explicit verification plan describing the unit tests, end-to-end scenarios, and logging artifacts required for follow-on implementation.","notes":"This bead is intentionally more specific than the general architecture design record. It focuses on real processor topology, cache locality, and node boundaries rather than only logical concurrency stages.

Operator sequencing:
1. Start with `bd-db300.5.6.1` for the placement map.
2. Then `bd-db300.5.6.2` for arena placement and handoff boundaries.
3. Then `bd-db300.5.6.3` for SMT sibling policy and remote-HITM budgets.
4. Treat this container as complete only when the placement map, allocator policy, and interference-sensitive budgets can be consumed mechanically by later verification beads.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:45:12.234249286Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-22T23:37:29.413326827Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","configuration","db300-program","db300-track-e","mechanical-sympathy","parallelism","track-e"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T00:26:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-22T23:22:49.849809522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
